topic: Claude Code Hook `if` Conditions — Comprehensive Analysis

resolvedContradictions: D7 Bash-only claim refuted by D3-S and G1 (all tool types confirmed)
D4 template syntax in `if` definitively debunked by G5 and GV2
D8 Windows path separator concern refuted for `if` field by GV1 (POSIX normalization confirmed)
D9 97% bypass rate corrected by G6 — figure was from pre-fix permission engine, not `if` conditions
D8 ensure-fnm.sh necessity vs D7-S redundancy resolved by lean wrapper Option B
openContradictions: Pipe OR within single `if` value — P14 uses it in practice but official docs recommend separate handlers
Compound command bypass post-v2.1.85 — whether `if` uses the same compound-aware engine as permissions is undocumented
MultiEdit `if` behavior — whether it matches when ANY or ALL files match is undocumented
PostToolUseFailure `if` stdin format — whether it receives original Bash command string for matching is assumed but unverified

themesIdentified: if field specification and supported events
ensure-fnm.sh as the largest avoidable overhead
gsd-context-monitor scoping options (template syntax definitively debunked)
Existing hook optimization (mostly ruled out — internal bail-outs already correct)
PreToolUse prevention gates (new — G3, 8 gates designed)
OTB feasibility triage (new — G4, 3 Tier 1, 4 Tier 2 ready)
New security/deploy/config hooks using if conditions
Cross-locale safety of if patterns (Windows POSIX normalization confirmed)
Compound command bypass risk correction (D9 overstated; practical risk LOW)

keyContradictions.description: D7 claimed `if` only works with Bash-matcher hooks
keyContradictions.resolution: D3-S and G1 confirmed wrong via official docs — `if` works with all tool types
G5 and GV2 confirmed definitively NOT supported — static matcher, no template expressions

keyContradictions.description: D8 implied ensure-fnm.sh is necessary; D7-S measured it as redundant on this machine
keyContradictions.resolution: Both correct at different scope — wrapper needed as safety net but redundant on HOME locale
keyContradictions.winner: Both (lean wrapper Option B is the reconciled recommendation)

keyContradictions.description: D9 claimed HIGH confidence in 97.9% compound bypass rate for `if` conditions; V1 downgraded to MEDIUM
keyContradictions.resolution: G6 confirmed: figure was from pre-fix permission engine (GitHub #4956), not `if` field; practical risk for this project is LOW (3-layer defense)

keyContradictions.description: D8-locale warned file-path `if` patterns fail on Windows; D1-spec claimed POSIX normalization
keyContradictions.resolution: GV1 confirmed via official docs: POSIX normalization applies to `if` evaluation engine; D8 concern valid only for hook script internals (stdin/args)

primaryActionableItems: Add .env.local.encrypted inline block to settings.json (5 min, zero risk, immediate ship)
Add broad matcher to gsd-context-monitor (5 min config change) — template syntax debunked, matcher is the only viable approach
Replace ensure-fnm.sh with lean wrapper (after WORK locale PATH verification)
Implement T1-1/T1-2/T1-3 next session (Governance Logger + Settings Guardian + Loop Detector, ~2 hrs total)
Implement PreToolUse Firestore rules guard (G3 Gate 2, 2-3 hrs, highest security value)
Add D6-D .claude/ config change alert inside post-write-validator.js monolith
debtItems: ensure-fnm.sh redundant overhead — DEBT-level drag on all hook performance
Dead code: isMarkdownFile and isConfigFile never used in post-write-validator.js
fnm not in install-cli-tools.sh — WORK locale hook infrastructure gap
settings.local.json committed to git but contains HOME-only permissions
No PostToolUseFailure hooks in project — gap in failure-loop detection
verifiedFindings: Hooks NOT hot-reloaded mid-session (GV1, official docs + empirical)
POSIX normalization before `if` matching on Windows (GV1, official docs)
Template syntax NOT supported in `if` fields (G5 + GV2, 5 GitHub issues confirm gap)
Permission engine IS compound-aware post-fix (GV2, official docs verbatim quote)
Exit code 2 = BLOCK for PreToolUse hooks (GV1, official docs)
PostToolUseFailure supports `if` conditions (GV2, D1-spec, official docs)
